message:
fix(cli,storage): resolve --no-db update "Issue not found" and WAL sidecar corruption (#229, #228)

Issue #229: `br --no-db update <id>` returned "Issue not found" while
direct storage.update_issue() calls succeeded on the same ID.

Root cause: frankensqlite's query_row_with_params prepared-statement
fast path caches root-page references that become stale after the
bulk DELETE + INSERT cycle during JSONL import. Two fixes:

1. Skip auto_import_storage_ctx_if_stale in --no-db mode — the
   in-memory DB was just populated from JSONL, so staleness detection
   is meaningless and the probe was actively poisoning the cache.

2. Convert all remaining query_row_with_params calls that can return
   zero rows to query_with_params + .into_iter().next(). The COUNT(*)
   calls are left alone since they always return exactly one row.
   Affected: get_parent_id, get_config, get_export_hash, get_metadata,
   find_by_external_ref, find_by_content_hash, is_tombstone,
   fetch_comment.

Issue #228: After `br sync --import-only`, `br show` failed with
"WAL file is corrupt: WAL file too small for header during rebuild".

Root cause: a 0-byte or truncated WAL sidecar file left on disk after
import. Fix: proactively remove WAL files < 32 bytes (the WAL header
size) before opening the connection. This is safe because SQLite
recreates the WAL on the next write and the main DB already contains
all committed data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
